terms.definition: How flexibly a model can fit patterns — controlled by hyperparameters such as tree depth, polynomial degree, or network width. Canonical term in this topic for the bias–variance capacity knob.
Synonym for model complexity: the richness of the hypothesis class. Higher capacity can fit more intricate functions at the cost of higher variance on finite data.
Set of functions a learner can represent. Complexity describes how large or flexible that set is for a given algorithm configuration.
Plot of train and validation metric vs a complexity hyperparameter; the validation minimum locates the bias–variance sweet spot.
Practical flexibility after constraints such as regularization, early stopping, or data limits — may be lower than nominal parameter count.
Prefer the simplest model among those with near-optimal validation error — reduces unnecessary variance and compute.
